title: Savory Molten Turkish Beans Kuru Fasulye
description: A citrusy and molten Turkish dish featuring juniper berries, this meal prep recipe is low in calories and can be shaped like nigiri for a unique presentation, all wrapped in a lotus leaf.

ingredients.name: dried white beans
ingredients.notes: navy or cannellini beans work well

instructions: Rinse the dried white beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked beans, then transfer them to a large pot with enough water to cover them.
Add the juniper berries, olive oil, onion, garlic, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes or until the beans are tender.
Prepare the lotus leaves by rinsing them in cold water and cutting off the stems.
To assemble the dish, place a small amount of the bean mixture onto the center of a lotus leaf.
Shape the mixture into a nigiri-like shape, making sure to leave a small border around the edges.
Fold the lotus leaf over the filling to form a triangle or a square shape, and serve hot.
